text.title: Mandatory Rules and AI-Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Concrete rules and common LLM mistakes for Eloquent ORM usage in Laravel.

list.item: Disable lazy loading in non-production: Model::preventLazyLoading(!$this->app->isProduction()) — makes N+1 throw instead of silently slow.
Always pair a model change with migration + factory + resource; agents that touch only the model cause drift visible in CI hours later.
Use firstOrCreate / updateOrCreate over manual where-save patterns to avoid race conditions with concurrent writers.
Resource classes are the API contract; never return raw models from controllers — leaked password_hash, remember_token, soft-delete columns.
Index every where/orderBy column in migrations; Eloquent makes it cheap to query unindexed columns but the bill arrives in production.

antipattern.description: $user->postss returns null without raising — magic relation typos pass static analysis.
antipattern.reason: Run php artisan ide-helper:models -W after every model change to generate _ide_helper_models.php; Larastan needs it to catch property typos.
Nested eager loading can load the entire object graph. Cap nesting depth at 2 in code review.

antipattern.description: Column added to migration without updating $fillable; User::create($validated) silently drops the field.
antipattern.reason: Mass assignment guard ($fillable) must be updated alongside every new column; agents frequently forget the $fillable update.

antipattern.description: unique('email') added on a soft-deleted model without a partial index.
antipattern.reason: Re-creating a previously soft-deleted user fails. Use unique('email')->whereNull('deleted_at') (Postgres) or restore-on-conflict logic.

antipattern.description: Tests run on sqlite but app ships to MySQL/Postgres.
antipattern.reason: JSON_EXTRACT, full-text, and case-sensitivity differ. Force the test suite onto the production DB engine in CI.
